title: Macros used in preprocessor directives should be defined before use
description: Macros used in preprocessor directives should be defined before useAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugbased-on-misrapreprocessorWhy is this an issue?More InfoAn attempt to use an undefined identifier may elicit a warning from the preprocessor. Or it may not; the preprocessor may simply assume that the undefined token has a value of 0.Therefore macro identifiers should not be used in preprocessor directives until after they have been defined, and this limited usage should be enforced with the use of definition tests.Noncompliant code example#if x > 0 /* x assumed to be zero if not defined */ #include SOMETHING_IMPORTANT #endif #ifdef y /* Okay; y is not evaluated */ #if y > 0 /* Okay; y must be defined to reach this point */ ... #endif #endifCompliant solution#define x 10
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
// Using undefined macro in preprocessor directive
#if FEATURE_ENABLED > 0 // FEATURE_ENABLED not defined, assumed 0
#include "feature.h"
#endif

#if DEBUG_LEVEL >= 2 // DEBUG_LEVEL undefined
#define VERBOSE_LOGGING
#endif
compliant:
// Define macros before use
#define FEATURE_ENABLED 1

#if FEATURE_ENABLED > 0
#include "feature.h"
#endif

// Use defined() operator for existence check
#ifdef DEBUG_LEVEL
#if DEBUG_LEVEL >= 2
#define VERBOSE_LOGGING
#endif
#endif

// Or use compound condition
#if defined(DEBUG_LEVEL) && (DEBUG_LEVEL >= 2)
#define VERBOSE_LOGGING
#endif

checks.message: Macros used in preprocessor directives should be defined before use. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
